name: Savoy cabbage (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Savoy cabbage (Brassica oleracea var. sabauda) [Vegetables] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DTU, PHENOL EXPLORER, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170388 (Cabbage, savoy, raw) - full nutrient panel
USDA FoodData Central FDC 169975 (Cabbage, raw) - free-sugar ratio used to apportion savoy total sugars
Sousa, Valentao, Andrade et al., Brassica oleracea organic acids by HPLC-UV (Eur Food Res Technol doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0; PMID 16277412; PMID 19722523)
Sauerkraut/cabbage-juice HPLC literature (~6 mM malate) for malic-acid FW estimate
Oxalate composition surveys (cabbage low-oxalate <10 mg/100g FW; retention study doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2011.09.005; cabbage powder PMC9737565)
Savoy-cabbage phytochemical study (ActaHortic doi:10.17660/ActaHortic.2012.939.38): vitamin C ~49, beta-carotene ~0.37, lutein ~0.18 mg/100g
FooDB FOOD00002 (source of original component list)
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe growing on savoy cabbage are the near-equimolar free hexoses (glucose ~1.2 + fructose ~1.0 g/100g) plus the organic acids malic and citric (co-dominant in Brassica oleracea). The original FooDB profile listed zero free sugars and zero organic acids - the largest growth-relevant gap - and mis-included retinol, oxypeucedanin, polylysine and D-serine. USDA amino-acid/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid values are high-confidence; organic-acid amounts are literatu
curation_added: D-glucose, D-fructose, sucrose, maltose (specific fermentable sugars with amounts; FooDB profile had none)
L-malic acid (co-dominant organic acid; primary organic-acid carbon source)
citric acid (co-dominant organic acid; was entirely absent)
oxalic, fumaric, succinic, quinic, shikimic, aconitic acids
L-phenylalanine and L-valine (amino acids missing from FooDB profile)
vitamin K, vitamin E, biotin (B7), lutein, palmitic/stearic acid, dietary fiber, selenium

curation_removed.name: Retinol
curation_removed.reason: Preformed retinol (animal-form vitamin A) does not occur in a plant food; savoy provides provitamin A as beta-carotene. FooDB mis-assignment.

curation_removed.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
curation_removed.reason: Furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/Rutaceae, not Brassicaceae; biologically implausible in cabbage. FooDB predicted/misassigned noise.

curation_removed.name: epsilon-Polylysine
curation_removed.reason: Microbially produced food preservative (Streptomyces), not a native cabbage metabolite. FooDB noise.

curation_removed.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
curation_removed.reason: Stereochemical artifact/duplicate of L-isoleucine; not a distinct nutrient.

curation_removed.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
curation_removed.reason: Trace flavor aldehyde, not a growth nutrient; negligible and non-fermentable.

curation_removed.name: D-Serine
curation_removed.reason: Plant protein contains L-serine, not the D-enantiomer; replaced with L-serine (USDA value). FooDB stereochemical mis-annotation.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170388 (total sugars 2.27 g) apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 'Cabbage, raw' free-sugar profile (glu 1.67/fru 1.45/suc 0.08 of 3.20 g)

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170388 total sugars apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 free-sugar ratio

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170388 total sugars apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 (sucrose 0.08/3.20 g)

components.name: maltose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170388 total sugars apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 (maltose 0.01/3.20 g)

components.name: L-malic acid
components.evidence_source: Cabbage juice HPLC literature ~6 mM malate (Fleming/sauerkraut studies) => ~0.08 g/100g FW; malic identified/quantified in Brassica oleracea leaves by Sousa & Valentao (doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0)

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence_source: Sousa, Valentao et al., Brassica oleracea organic-acid HPLC-UV (doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0; PMID 16277412)

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence_source: Oxalate food-composition surveys: cabbage classified low-oxalate <10 mg/100g FW (Guinea Lynx / oxalatecontent.com; retention study doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2011.09.005)

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence_source: Sousa & Valentao, Brassica oleracea organic acids (doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0)

components.name: quinic acid
components.evidence_source: Sousa/Valentao Brassica oleracea var. acephala/costata organic acids (PMID 19722523)

components.name: vitamin C
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170388 (savoy 31 mg; savoy-specific study reports ~49 mg, doi:10.17660/ActaHortic.2012.939.38)

components.name: biotin
components.evidence_source: not in USDA FDC 170388; present in Brassica vegetables at low ug/100g levels

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 170388 (savoy-specific ~370 ug reported by doi:10.17660/ActaHortic.2012.939.38)

components.name: chloride
components.evidence_source: FooDB profile; genuine anion in cabbage tissue

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence_source: FooDB profile; sulfur source (also glucosinolate-bound S)

components.name: nickel
components.evidence_source: FooDB profile; trace micronutrient in plants

uncovered.name: aconitic acid
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

uncovered.name: glucosinolates (sinigrin, glucobrassicin - defining Brassica phytochemicals, no BiGG met)

oxygen_note: food substrate; O2 availability is a simulation condition, not a property of the food
